cross_lingual_assessment: Does not generalize as hypothesized; both languages suggest hedging may capture encyclopedic caution rather than contestation.
results_discussion_use: Discuss as negative/diagnostic finding rather than support for H1.

german_model_variant_available: Unavailable in final_features_de.csv; omitted equally from available DE comparison
cross_lingual_assessment: Partial generalization in direction only; English is significant and stable, German original is directionally right but weak and F2 robustness could only run Hyland-only because SentiWS files are absent.
results_discussion_use: Use English as main support; report German as inconclusive and note SentiWS reproducibility limitation.

cross_lingual_assessment: Generalizes directionally; German improves when generic quantifiers are removed, but remains weaker than English after Holm correction.
results_discussion_use: Include as cross-lingual partial support and as example of language-specific lexicon refinement.

cross_lingual_assessment: Strongest cross-lingual feature; German original suffered sparsity/NaNs but expanded smoothed ratio improved coverage and Holm-significant signal.
results_discussion_use: Use prominently in Results and Discussion as best generalizing feature with German robustness improvement.

cross_lingual_assessment: Language/model sensitive; German revision reduces saturation and improves F1 contribution but preserves the wrong direction.
results_discussion_use: Report as robustness/method limitation, not core confirmatory evidence.

cross_lingual_assessment: Generalizes directionally; English significant, German weaker but important in improved available logistic model.
results_discussion_use: Include as moderate cross-lingual support, emphasizing German is classification-useful but not Holm-significant.

cross_lingual_assessment: Generalizes directionally but German effect is weak/non-significant.
results_discussion_use: Use as English-supported finding with weaker German replication.
